good.text: Says it does not sell your personal data.

clauses.label: No Data Sale
clauses.summary: The clause says the company never sells personal information to anyone, protecting users from data sales.
clauses.excerpt:
## Designed to keep you

private, safe, and secure

[See how we build for safety](./../../../intl/en_us/safety/)

-

Play silent looping video Pause silent looping video

Privacy isn’t a feature, it’s a foundation. That’s why we uphold responsible data practices in everything we build — like never selling your personal information, to anyone. And with easy-to-use tools, you can choose the privacy settings that are right for you.

[Learn more](./../../../intl/en_us/safety/)

-   All Google products are secure by default. Whether you’re sending an email in Gmail or planning your next vacation wi

history.summary: Taxonomy update: 7 clause(s) re-evaluated under the current scoring rules. The document text did not change.

The company updated its privacy policy by making changes to three sections and removing one section. The changes involve revisions to how user data is handled and protected, but do not add new requirements for collecting user information. No user-hostile changes were found. These updates aim to provide clearer guidelines on data usage and user rights.

The company confirms it does not sell personal information to anyone and provides users with privacy controls to manage their data. This means users have some control over their information and can expect it to be kept private. No user-hostile changes were found in this clause.
